description: Arrow is a small (~2200 LOC), well-tested Python date/time library with a rich history of similar, recurring bug classes: locale humanize errors, date parsing edge cases, DST handling, and token formatting bugs. It has excellent test coverage (tests/test_arrow.py, tests/test_locales.py, tests/test_parser.py), is Python-only, and has dozens of closed bugs fixed with 1-3 file changes mostly in arrow/arrow.py, arrow/locales.py, or arrow/parser.py. The bug category is extremely homogeneous — almost all bugs are variations of 'humanize/locale output is wrong' or 'parsing edge case produces wrong result', making it ideal for a coding+review agent worktrial.
bug_category: humanize/locale output bugs and date parsing edge cases in arrow/locales.py, arrow/arrow.py, and arrow/parser.py

issues.title: humanize reports '16 days' as 'a month' instead of '2 weeks'
issues.body_summary: humanize() incorrectly reports a 16-day difference as 'in a month' instead of 'in 2 weeks' due to a logic error in calendar_diff handling.

issues.title: Incorrect plural for seconds in Russian (locale: ru_RU)
issues.body_summary: humanize() produces incorrect Russian plural forms for 'seconds' due to a missing or wrong plural rule in RussianLocale.

issues.title: No support for quarter granularity in Russian (locale: ru_RU)
issues.body_summary: humanize() with granularity='quarter' raises ValueError for Russian locale because quarter timeframe was not defined in RussianLocale.

issues.title: Humanize doesn't support 'quarter' for granularity
issues.body_summary: humanize() with granularity=['quarter'] raised an error because quarter granularity was not implemented in arrow.py.

issues.title: Empty List of Granularities For Humanize Doesn't Raise ValueError
issues.body_summary: Passing an empty list to humanize(granularity=[]) should raise a ValueError but instead silently produces wrong output.

issues.title: Humanize Format Relative Error For Multiple Granularities
issues.body_summary: humanize() with multiple granularities including 'second' returns incorrect output due to a bug in the relative time formatting for Slavic locales.

issues.title: Czech/Slovak Locales Humanize Error When Zero Delta
issues.body_summary: humanize() raises an error for Czech and Slovak locales when the time delta is zero because dehumanize support was missing for those locales.

issues.title: Floats in humanize output for languages overriding _format_timeframe
issues.body_summary: humanize() produces float values (e.g. '2.0 hours') instead of integers for locales that override _format_timeframe method.

issues.title: humanize() rounding problem
issues.body_summary: humanize() with granularity='auto' uses incorrect boundary limits causing a 30-day diff to be reported as 'a month' vs '4 weeks'.

issues.title: Week granularity not found in Czech locale
issues.body_summary: humanize() with granularity='week' raises ValueError for Czech locale because 'week' and 'weeks' timeframes were missing from CzechLocale.

issues.title: Korean locale broken when humanize() uses 'n weeks' timeframe
issues.body_summary: humanize() raises ValueError for Korean locale when the result should be expressed in weeks because 'week'/'weeks' timeframes were not defined.

issues.title: humanize granularity doesn't behave as expected
issues.body_summary: humanize() with granularity=['seconds'] returns wrong output (e.g., '0 seconds' for a 200-second difference) due to incorrect singular/plural handling.

issues.title: .humanize() leads to wrong result with datetime.date
issues.body_summary: humanize() produces wrong results (e.g., '6 hours ago') when called on an Arrow object created from a naive datetime.date because timezone comparison goes wrong.

issues.title: humanize has odd behavior with future days
issues.body_summary: humanize() with granularity='day' returns 'in a day' for durations that are 2+ days in the future due to an off-by-one in future day boundaries.

issues.title: "dddd" and "ddd" tokens no longer support different capitalisations from 0.15.8
issues.body_summary: Parsing day-of-week tokens (ddd/dddd) became case-sensitive in 0.15.8, causing failures for inputs like 'monday' or 'MONDAY' where only 'Monday' worked.

issues.title: Range gives inconsistent results for month end
issues.body_summary: arrow.Arrow.range() and span_range() produce incorrect results at the end of month/quarter/year frames, returning dates outside the expected range.

issues.title: Problem with differences between time points during DST transition
issues.body_summary: Arrow raises an exception or produces wrong results when computing differences across DST transitions involving imaginary datetimes.

issues.title: Error parsing date with over 999999 in microseconds
issues.body_summary: Parsing a timestamp string with more than 6 fractional-second digits raises 'microsecond must be in 0..999999' instead of truncating correctly.

issues.title: Make .get() handle string tzinfo kwarg properly
issues.body_summary: arrow.get() with a string tzinfo keyword argument raises an error instead of properly resolving the timezone.

issues.title: "a" token for am/pm is not being processed in get() when using Spanish locale
issues.body_summary: Parsing a time string with Spanish locale fails to interpret the 'a' (am/pm) token because meridians were missing from the Spanish locale definition.

issues.title: HH token does not support the number 24 (midnight at end of day)
issues.body_summary: Parsing times with HH=24 (valid in some formats to represent midnight at end of day) raises a ValueError instead of being handled gracefully.

issues.title: Parsing gives incorrect data instead of failing for unknown ISO format
issues.body_summary: Parsing ISO 8601 week-date strings like '2001W05' returns a wrong date (2001-05-01) instead of raising a parse error.

issues.title: YY token maps 2018 to year 20 instead of 2018
issues.body_summary: The YY token (2-digit year) was incorrectly mapped to 2020 as '20' rather than properly interpreting '18' as 2018.

issues.title: Fix humanize month limits (incorrect boundary between weeks and months)
issues.body_summary: humanize() incorrectly transitions from 'weeks' to 'months' at the wrong number of days due to an off-by-one in month boundary calculation.

issues.title: String escape doesn't work in arrow format strings
issues.body_summary: Using escape brackets [] in arrow format strings to prevent token substitution does not work correctly, causing literal text to be interpreted as tokens.

baseline_note: Commit just before the oldest fix in this issue set (pre 88de0faf, 2019-04-26). All 25 issues are unfixed at this point.
